design_decisions.d0_open_oeq_residuals: Open-ended question(s) left as the note's acknowledged open problem for D0.5 review: oeq:full-beta-frontier
design_decisions.d0r_pending_approval: D0.R made 0 assumption + 1 definition change(s) PROVISIONALLY (pending approval, direction-of-truth align/correct/discharge): def:beta-frontier-handle
design_decisions.f25_upper_route_prune: F2.5 scaffold gate (10-round non-convergence, escalated fix-source) caused by the deferred upper-route HOIF/sieve/local-polynomial source-lemma cluster (nodes L-8..L-15: lem:hoif-projection-risk-source, lem:hoif-remainder-product-source, lem:series-sieve-l2-source, lem:local-polynomial-window-source, lem:bounded-sieve-local-polynomial-rates, lem:localized-projection-hoif-risk-decomposition, lem:hoif-order-remainder-negligible, lem:same-class-hoif-upper-{smooth,deficient}-covariates, lem:truncation-reciprocal-lipschitz). The note labels these 'External comparator only / source-side scope caveat — NOT a same-class certified upper theorem' (L-11..L-15), and they are ORPHAN (no headline T-block / Frontier / proof depends on them; all 22 graph edges run dead->live, 0 live->dead). The F2 scaffolder realized this external-comparator prose as same-class estimator-class Lean predicates (IsProjectionHOIFEstimator etc.) which can only over-claim (arbitrary unconstrained basis) or be vacuous (BK rate moved into an assumed hUpper hypothesis), so the gate kept flagging drift and could never converge — faithfully stating them requires the unbuilt I-1..I-4 substrate (a separate paper's HOIF theory). RESOLUTION (meta-orchestrator hand intervention, run stopped): pruned the 10 orphan nodes + their edges from graph.json and deleted Helpers/UpperRoute{Source,Scope,Truncation}.lean; the BK upper rate remains faithfully represented as the published comparator def:published-hoif-rate (rho_n) plus the rate-identity algebra (lem:rho-oracle-regime-algebra / lem:rho-deficient-regime-algebra), which the smooth/deficient regime theorems use to certify sharpness-at-the-exponent where s>=d/4. Headline claims UNCHANGED (all-beta lower floor + sharpness where it matches + oeq:full-beta-frontier open). Also added missing @realizes S3 tags (alpha,beta,s,M,c_0,t_0,epsilon_0,rho_n,R_n) and the O_1,...,O_n n-sample tag in Basic.lean (the other F2.5 blocker: untagged setup symbols). This is a faithful scope/literature clarification, NOT a weakening — the paper never claimed a same-class upper bound; it cites Bonvini-Kennedy 2022 for the upper side.
design_decisions.lower_bound_bretagnolle_huber_gate: Phase B proof-fill on lem:le-cam-two-point-mse-source escalated build-substrate: the Le Cam two-point reduction needs a positive testing-error floor for an ARBITRARY finite KL budget K. Causalean has only the Pinsker form (klForm_two_point_lower_bound, positive only for K<2). The NOTE's own proof_tex explicitly invokes the Bretagnolle-Huber/Le Cam inequality (Tsybakov 2009 Thm 2.2 route) for arbitrary K, so narrowing to K<2 + Pinsker would weaken the note's stated lemma and deviate from its cited proof (forbidden direction). Decision (user: 'you decide'): provide Bretagnolle-Huber as an honest documented SUBSTRATE-GATE (CausalSmith/Mathlib/InformationTheory/BretagnolleHuber.lean: bretagnolle_huber_affinity, 1-tvDist >= 1/2 exp(-KL), sorry, logged in SUBSTRATE_DEBT.md as the sibling of the resolved PinskerBound). BH is a TRUE classical named 1979 inequality and a general-purpose TOOL, not this paper's crux; the novel crux (the two-point Holder-bump construction + the proven bernoulli_mean_channel_kl + the estimation->testing reduction) is proven. The lower bound therefore banks 'modulo the classical Bretagnolle-Huber gate', exactly parallel to the upper-route HOIF gates (modulo Bonvini-Kennedy). Full BH proof is a high-value, self-contained follow-up substrate build (needs the Bhattacharyya-affinity layer absent from Mathlib). le_cam imports BH + carries a precise proof-route hint; the filler closes le_cam (sorry-free MODULO the BH gate) + oracle on resume.
design_decisions.lower_bound_construction_laundering_caug: REJECTED a laundered oracle construction (codex Phase-B build). codex disclosed in its own caveat that the DoseLaw model leaves mu/pot as FREE fields untied to dataMeasure: it gave both two-point witnesses a common Measure.dirac data law (Y=0,A=t0,X=0) with pot=0, carrying the bump ONLY in the free mu field that thetaFunctional reads, so the n-fold KL was proved as 0 and the 'separation' was a disconnected mu artifact — a vacuous bound over a non-causal class. Verified in Witness.lean:44-51 (doseWitness dataMeasure := Measure.dirac, pot := 0). Quarantined to Helpers/.laundered_quarantine/, reverted oracle to sorry. ROOT CAUSE: HolderDoseClass/thetaFunctional do not enforce mu = E_dataMeasure[Y|A,X] (the F2.5 reviewer matched the defs on surface form, missing the semantic tie). GENUINE FIX (required, not gateable — this is the crux): (1) tighten HolderDoseClass to enforce mu = conditional regression of Y given (A,X) under dataMeasure, so minimaxRisk is the true causal risk and thetaFunctional = E[Y(t0)]; (2) build genuine witnesses whose dataMeasure is the real joint law X~p0 x A|X~q0 x Y|A,X~twoPointMean(mu_zeta), giving real bump-driven KL via bernoulli_mean_channel_kl + tensorization; (3) re-run F2.5->F4. This is hard measure-theory (the joint law construction codex dodged). Lower bound is NOT done — honest status: regime theorems + le_cam (modulo BH) + rate algebra + bernoulli KL band are real; the construction is the open crux.
design_decisions.bh_gate_misstated_fix_at_discharge: The BH substrate-gate bretagnolle_huber_affinity is currently MIS-STATED: the bare `∀` probability-measure form `½·exp(-(klDiv μ ν).toReal) ≤ 1 - tvDist μ ν` is FALSE when μ⊥ν (klDiv=⊤, (⊤).toReal=0 ⇒ LHS=½; tvDist=1 ⇒ RHS=0; ½≤0 false) — the same μ≪ν∧klDiv≠⊤ side-condition trap as PinskerBound. A BH subagent is building the CORRECT theorem in Causalean/Stat/Minimax/BretagnolleHuber.lean (mirroring pinskerBound_of_ac_of_ne_top). At DISCHARGE: replace the CausalSmith gate with the correct-statement reuse of the Causalean lemma, re-verify le_cam supplies the side-condition from its finite-KL-budget hypothesis (klDiv ≤ ENNReal.ofReal K ⇒ klDiv≠⊤ ⇒ μ≪ν via klDiv_ne_top_iff), then rewind→F2.5 so F2.5–F4 re-pass review with the discharged substrate (per user: discharge+rewind-to-F2.5, not F1).
design_decisions.lower_bound_genuinely_proven: GENUINE construction CLOSED + verified: oracle_dose_regression_lower_all_beta builds, #print axioms=[propext,Classical.choice,Quot.sound,sorryAx]; sorryAx only from 2 honest gates (bretagnolle_huber_affinity = PROVEN in Causalean, needs rewire; doseBump_holder_gate = classical ContDiffBump Holder estimate). Anti-laundering CONFIRMED: doseWitness_muReg (E[Y|A,X]=mu tie) PROVEN, KL genuinely bump-driven (int psi^2 <= 2(M-eta0)h, nonzero) NOT 0, oracle stmt unchanged, genuine Measure.bind joint law. The Dirac-shortcut laundering is structurally impossible (tightened class). Remaining for fully-sorry-free: discharge BH (rewire+le_cam side-conds), build doseBump_holder_gate, Phase E upper-gate instantiation from I-1..I-4.

added_assumptions.label: ass:baseline-submodel-slack
added_assumptions.statement: There exist a density p_0 on [0,1]^d, a conditional treatment density q_0 on [0,1], and a constant eta_0>0 such that p_0 and q_0 have Holder norms at most M-eta_0 in the smoothness classes required of p_{X,P} and pi_P, q_0(a) >= c_0+eta_0 for all a in [t_0-epsilon_0,t_0+epsilon_0], p_0 is bounded above by M-eta_0, and there exists B_0 in (0,M) such that the constant-zero outcome regression admits a symmetric two-point channel supported on {-B_0,B_0} inside [-M,M].

added_assumptions.label: ass:consistency
added_assumptions.statement: Y = Y(A) almost surely under P.

added_assumptions.label: ass:no-unmeasured-confounding
added_assumptions.statement: For every a in [0,1], Y(a) is independent of A conditional on X under P.

added_assumptions.label: ass:mu-is-regression
added_assumptions.statement: HolderDoseClass member MuIsRegression: E_dataMeasure[Y|(A,X)] =ᵃᵉ mu_P(A,X) (mu is the data's actual conditional regression).
added_assumptions.rationale: The note's thetaFunctional docstring already CLAIMS '= E_P[Y(t0)] under consistency+ignorability', which REQUIRES these ties; the original scaffold left mu/px as free fields, permitting a vacuous (Dirac) two-point witness. Adding the ties makes minimaxRisk the genuine CAUSAL risk and thetaFunctional the genuine causal value, and is what STRUCTURALLY forbids the laundering. Headline statements UNCHANGED; the genuine witness PROVES both ties. Note def:holder-dose-class to be synced to 13 members at papersmith P1.

added_assumptions.label: ass:px-is-x-density
added_assumptions.statement: HolderDoseClass member PxIsXDensity: dataMeasure.map X = (volume|cube).withDensity px (px is the data's X-marginal density).
added_assumptions.rationale: The note's thetaFunctional docstring already CLAIMS '= E_P[Y(t0)] under consistency+ignorability', which REQUIRES these ties; the original scaffold left mu/px as free fields, permitting a vacuous (Dirac) two-point witness. Adding the ties makes minimaxRisk the genuine CAUSAL risk and thetaFunctional the genuine causal value, and is what STRUCTURALLY forbids the laundering. Headline statements UNCHANGED; the genuine witness PROVES both ties. Note def:holder-dose-class to be synced to 13 members at papersmith P1.

added_assumptions.label: ass:pi-is-cond-treatment-density
added_assumptions.statement: HolderDoseClass member PiIsCondTreatmentDensity: 0 ≤ pi_P on [0,1]×cube AND dataMeasure.map (A,X) = (Leb|[0,1] ⊗ Leb|cube).withDensity (pi_P·px) (pi is the data's genuine conditional treatment density A|X, pinned to the law by the joint factorization).
added_assumptions.source: pipeline localPatch (F2.5 scaffold-mismatch self-heal; genuine-construction anti-laundering)
added_assumptions.rationale: Third sibling of ass:mu-is-regression / ass:px-is-x-density. The note's thetaFunctional docstring CLAIMS '= E_P[Y(t0)] under consistency+ignorability', which requires pi to be the actual conditional A|X density; the original scaffold left P.pi a free field constrained only by Holder/positivity, permitting a class strictly BROADER than the paper's (lower bound over a broader class is a weaker claim). Adding the joint-factorization tie pins pi to the law, making minimaxRisk the genuine causal risk over the paper's class. Headline statements UNCHANGED; the genuine witness PROVES the tie. def:holder-dose-class to be synced to 14 members at papersmith P1.

missing_architecture_items.purpose: Interior local-polynomial / kernel regression estimator with its bias = O(h^eta) and stochastic L2 size = O((Nh)^{-1/2}) theory (Fan-Gijbels 1996; Tsybakov 2009), so lem:local-polynomial-window-source and the smoothing-bias conjunct of lem:hoif-projection-risk-source can bound the ESTIMATOR risk rather than the deterministic proxy kernelSmoothingBias.
missing_architecture_items.why_substantial: Check 1 (parent abstraction): no POSystem/POManski/SWIGGraph/SCM.Do or any Causalean estimator-risk abstraction carries an estimator-bias/variance statement; the source lemma is about an estimator's risk, which no parent supplies (lean_local_search localPolynomial -> []). Check 2 (proof-internal construction): the bias/variance is not assembled from existing primitives by conditioning/finite-indexing — it is the conclusion of classical equivalent-kernel expansions and design-density variance calculations absent from substrate. Check 3 (single Mathlib helper): it is a multi-lemma theory (estimator def + interior bias bound + variance bound + componentwise J-coefficient variance), a >=3-lemma API reused by lem:local-polynomial-window-source, lem:hoif-projection-risk-source, and lem:bounded-sieve-local-polynomial-rates (>=2 nodes), not one Mathlib-shaped lemma.

missing_architecture_items.purpose: Series/sieve L2 estimator with approximation property ||f-f_L||_2 <= C L^{-s/d} and least-squares prediction rate O(L^{-s/d}+sqrt(L/N)) for Holder functions/densities (Newey 1997; Chen 2007; BCCK 2015), so lem:series-sieve-l2-source bounds the ESTIMATOR's L2 prediction error rather than the proxy variance L*sigma2/N.
missing_architecture_items.why_substantial: Check 1: no parent abstraction provides a sieve least-squares estimator or its prediction risk (lean_local_search sieve -> only CategoryTheory.Sieve, unrelated). Check 2: the prediction rate is the conclusion of sieve approximation theory + regularized least-squares variance, not a proof-internal assembly of existing primitives. Check 3: it is a >=3-lemma module (sieve approximation lemma + prediction-rate lemma + density-projection variant) reused by lem:series-sieve-l2-source and lem:bounded-sieve-local-polynomial-rates (>=2 nodes).

missing_architecture_items.purpose: Order-m higher-order influence function projection-risk decomposition and product-remainder theory (Robins-Li-Tchetgen-vanderVaart 2008/2016): localized first-order variance O((nh)^{-1}), projection product bias^2 O(J^{-4s/d}), degenerate projected U-statistic variance O(J/(nh)^2), and the C*delta_n^{2(m+1)} product remainder, so lem:hoif-projection-risk-source and lem:hoif-remainder-product-source state the ESTIMATOR's risk components rather than sigma2/M-scaled proxies.
missing_architecture_items.why_substantial: Check 1: Causalean has only first/second-order orthogonal moments + DR remainder identities, no order-m HOIF/projection-risk abstraction (lean_local_search higherOrderInfluence -> []); no parent carries it. Check 2: the degenerate U-statistic variance and m+1-fold product remainder are conclusions of higher-order von Mises / U-statistic theory, not a finite-indexing construction over present primitives. Check 3: it is cluster-scale (projection-risk decomposition lemma + degenerate U-statistic variance lemma + product-remainder lemma), a >=3-lemma API reused by lem:hoif-projection-risk-source, lem:hoif-remainder-product-source, lem:localized-projection-hoif-risk-decomposition, and lem:hoif-order-remainder-negligible (>=2 nodes).

substrate_built.nl_statement: Bretagnolle-Huber: for prob measures with μ≪ν and klDiv≠⊤, ½·exp(-(klDiv μ ν).toReal) ≤ 1 - tvDist μ ν
Matrix-inverse/leverage concentration: Neumann-resolvent transport of (M^-1)_00 from population S + entrywise union-bound Chebyshev. Bounds |(M^-1)_00 - (S^-1)_00|.
Order-m HOIF projection-risk: degenerate-U variance Var<=C*J/(nh)^2 (zeta=J trace identity) + product-remainder bias^2<=C*delta^{2(m+1)}, assembled into the projection-risk decomposition.
(M^-1)_00 <= C/(Nh) and sqrt(M00*(M^-1)_00) <= C', given the kernel-moment factorization S=(Nh)(DTD) and density-constant inputs.

substrate_built.decl_name: Causalean.Stat.Nonparametric.localPoly_estimatorBias_window, localPoly_estimatorStochL2 (+ density-constant kernel factorization, square-completion leverage)
substrate_built.nl_statement: estimatorBias <= Cbias*h^beta and estimatorStochL2 <= Cvar*(Nh)^{-1/2}, with explicit density+kernel-moment constants; kernel change-of-vars S=(Nh)DTD discharged.
Conditional-on-good-design bias/variance lift to full sample law via condExp truncation + law of total variance (Mathlib integral_condVar_add_variance_condExp).
Jackson L2 approximation J^{-s/d} (piecewise-Taylor sieve, NO gate) + LS prediction rate O(J^{-s/d}+sqrt(J/N)).

substrate_built.gate_id: I-4 same-class HOIF upper rate COMPLETE
substrate_built.nl_statement: R_n <= C*rho_n (both regimes: smooth n^{-2a/(2a+1)}, deficient n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/a)}), 5-term HOIF risk optimized over h,J,m.

flags.f2_scaffold_directive:
MANUAL RESOLVED-OEQ SALVAGE (authoritative for this F2 rewind):
- oeq:full-beta-frontier was removed and replaced by proved thm:certified-partial-beta-frontier; emit a theorem named certifiedPartialBetaFrontier from the answer statement and proof in discovery/core.json.
Any legacy formalization.md prose that still presents one of those nodes as an open question is superseded by core.json and plan.json. Do not recreate an oeq: Prop def.

proposed_from.topic: Minimax lower bound for the interior continuous-treatment dose-response curve E[mu(t,X)] under anisotropic Holder smoothness (treatment-direction alpha, treatment-density beta, covariate-direction s), matching the published HOIF upper rate of Bonvini-Kennedy (arXiv:2207.11825); distinguished from incremental-effect/edge-dose continuous-exposure minimax bounds.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: thm:sharp-pointwise-lower-bound
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The lower-bound claim is accepted only on the beta >= alpha, common-covariate-smoothness, interior fixed-dose Holder subclass, not as the full anisotropic phase diagram.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Sharpness means matching the Bonvini-Kennedy HOIF upper rate on the stated beta >= alpha subclass.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: oeq:full-beta-frontier
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The beta<alpha frontier is explicitly left open and is not part of the settled kernel.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The proposal advances with the full beta-frontier treated as a named open-ended follow-on rather than a claimed theorem.

banked_reason: F5 clean: certified all-beta lower-floor theorem and regime comparison passed genuine F2.5/F4 review; unrestricted same-class upper frontier remains open.
